title: Status bubble thresholds and blink cadence
definition: Native DF's Hungry, Thirsty, and Drowsy chooser uses fixed unit-timer thresholds; how its GetTickCount windows publish into persistent overhead texture cells is corpus-disputed.

facts.statement: Hungry is eligible when counters2.hunger_timer is at least 50000.
Drowsy is eligible when counters2.sleepiness_timer is at least 57600.
Thirsty is eligible when counters2.thirst_timer is at least 25000.

The native chooser compares map_renderer.cur_tick_count modulo 1000 against [0,200) for Hungry, [300,500) for Drowsy, and [500,700) for Thirsty; ordinary fortress callers supply Windows GetTickCount milliseconds.

The current hypothesis that a need-bubble texture cell is visible only when the same recorded cur_tick_count phase is inside its chooser window is contradicted by retained Hungry and Thirsty cells across many out-of-window phases.
All three checks share a per-unit visibility gate and a no-higher-priority-indicator gate; the chooser is one if/else-if ladder, so the bubbles cannot draw simultaneously for one unit.

uncertainties.statement: The same function appears to use a soul field at offset 0x368 above 9999 for a stress-type bubble, but the field identity is not verified.
The same function appears to compare a focus computation involving soul offset 0x248 against 80 for a distraction-type indicator, but the field identity is not verified.
Several other indicators appear to use a 500-tick cycle with a [0, 100) window, but their identities are not verified.

Hungry and Thirsty designation-layer cells persisted for roughly two seconds. A retained or deferred viewport overlay is the strongest explanation, but the exact publish/clear event still needs instrumentation.

positive: Each timer at its exact threshold, plus direct chooser instrumentation at each GetTickCount window.
boundary: Each timer one below and exactly at threshold; each chooser-window start, final included millisecond, and excluded end millisecond.
counterexamples: Retained designation-layer cells outside the same-frame chooser window must remain distinct from a fresh chooser decision.

absenceGuards: Do not model the three chooser branches as simultaneously selectable for one unit.
Do not phase-suppress an already-published viewport bubble from cur_tick_count alone.

stateSlice: chooser invocation and viewport publish/clear event
requiredFrames: Viewport cell publication and clearing across retained phases
